name: Creme Constance
recipeInstructions: Despite the simplicity of this recipe, it is delightful and subtle, depending very much on the quality of the basic ingredients used. Melt butter and soften the finely chopped onion over gentle heat. When tender but not browned, add curry powder (preferably homemade) and continue cooking for 1-2 minutes. Stir in flour to make a roux, cooking for an additional minute or so. Remove from heat and let cool slightly. Gradually stir in stock and milk, then return to heat and stir until boiling. Boil briefly, then reduce the heat and simmer gently for about 15 minutes. Add coconut milk (or creamed coconut if milk is unavailable) and stir to incorporate. Strain if desired, adjust seasoning, and reheat. Serve with a little cream on top and a slice of lemon.
